title: 39/39 validation measures boundary-compliance not classification accuracy
evidence: compare-output.ts:64 skips code check when allowedCodes==UNRESOLVED_BY_AUTHOR (81 such markers); every axis has nullCodeAllowed=true and codeRequired=false; reproduced 39/39 PASS with 80/117 axis slots null and only 7 distinct codes ever emitted
expected_behavior: If 39/39 is cited as proof of methodological readiness, it should test that the engine produces methodologically-correct P/O/A codes
actual_behavior: Validation asserts the engine stays within author-specified boundaries and does not emit forbidden codes/inferences; it does not require any specific code and waives 68% of axis decisions
impact: '39/39 proves readiness' is overstated; passing is dominated by conservative restraint (null) plus negative checks
recommended_action: Re-label claim as 'boundary/contract compliance + determinism'; add accuracy cases with codeRequired where methodology is unambiguous; report UNRESOLVED rate

title: Expected allowedCodes are supersets fitted around engine output
evidence: For every coded case sampled (GEN-AIR-CANADA-759, ADV-VIOLATION-WITHOUT-AWARENESS, OFFICIAL-COMAIR-5191, GEN-NO-FAILURE) allowedCodes contained exactly the engine's emitted code(s) and nullCodeAllowed=true
expected_behavior: Expected boundaries should be authored independently from methodology, before observing engine output
actual_behavior: allowedCodes consistently equal engine-output ∪ adjacent code; structure consistent with expectations fitted to the engine (leakage between fixture and implementation)
impact: Reduces independence of the baseline; 'validation' partly circular
recommended_action: Document baseline authoring provenance; freeze an independently-authored gold set with codeRequired for unambiguous cases

title: Engine cannot reach 8 of 22 canonical leaf codes
evidence: decideP never returns SIM for P_CAPABILITY (P_TIME_PRESSURE/P_INFORMATION_* unreachable => P-D,P-E,P-F,P-G,P-H dead); decideA A_IMPLEMENTED never returns NÃO_FEEDBACK (A-B dead); A_TIME_PRESSURE never returns NÃO_SELECAO/NÃO_FEEDBACK (A-F,A-G dead). Confirmed by 39-case code tally: only 7 distinct codes ever emitted
expected_behavior: Decision heuristics should be able to reach every canonical leaf where evidence supports it
actual_behavior: 8 of 22 leaf codes are structurally unreachable by the decision logic
impact: Engine cannot propose entire families of failure modes (attention-under-time-pressure, feedback failure, etc.); bounds methodological coverage
reproducibility: High - static code analysis + runtime tally
recommended_action: Add decision branches for the missing transitions or document the reduced active code set as an explicit engine limitation

title: Objective-axis violation over-attribution from mere continuation
evidence: decideO O_RULES routes 'continue/continued/despite warning/unstable approach/below profile' to NÃO (violation path); O_ROUTINE then returns SIM (same continuation keywords) => O-B routine violation without awareness/conscious-deviation evidence. 39-case tally: O-B emitted 18 times; ADV-VIOLATION-WITHOUT-AWARENESS emits O-B and PASSES
expected_behavior: Violation codes (O-B/O-C) require explicit awareness of a known rule and conscious deviation; continuation under perception failure should not be classified as a violation
actual_behavior: Continuation of an unsafe approach is auto-classified as a routine violation (O-B) from keyword presence, without awareness evidence
impact: Risk of mis-attributing perception/objective-error events to the violation axis; harness does not catch it
recommended_action: Require explicit awareness/conscious-deviation evidence before O-B/O-C; route bare continuation to UNRESOLVED or objective-error path

title: P/O/A decision logic is English-only while product runs Portuguese narratives
evidence: All node-decision regexes in evaluate-node.ts are English (recognized/did not/wrong runway/go-around). create-analysis.ts:27 sets engine locale 'pt-BR'. Escape-window detection IS bilingual (candidate-escape-window.ts) but axis decisions are not
expected_behavior: Engine should classify the language the product accepts (pt-BR)
actual_behavior: Portuguese narratives match almost no axis pattern => axes return UNRESOLVED; real product analyses will mostly produce all-null candidate codes
impact: Core classification capability does not function for the product's actual input language; undermines the 'A IA classifica' value proposition
recommended_action: Add Portuguese lexical patterns (or language-agnostic extraction) to axis decisions; gate copy on real PT capability

title: Guardrail assurance flags are hardcoded false (vacuous)
evidence: 10-assurance.ts:68-75 hardcodes consequenceUsedAsCause/postEscapeHuntingDetected/oeUsed/inventedQuestionDetected/preconditionUsedAsEscapePoint = false; only actorMigrationDetected is computed. compare-output.ts:146 asserts these flags==false (always true)
expected_behavior: Guardrail flags should be computed from engine behavior so downstream checks are meaningful
actual_behavior: Five of six guardrail flags are static false; the 39-case requiredGuardrails checks pass vacuously
impact: False sense of methodological assurance; guardrail-based test assertions prove nothing
recommended_action: Compute guardrail flags from actual traversal/evidence or remove them and the assertions that rely on them

title: Live user classification path is legacy /api/analyze not the frozen vNext engine
evidence: Risk profile reads legacy analyses.perception_code/objective_code/action_code (server.ts:363); events/new flow + credits gate (analyze/route.ts:174) feed legacy analyses; vNext product beta is admin-only behind SERA_VNEXT_PRODUCT_BETA_ENABLED
expected_behavior: Candidate-only locks and methodology freeze should govern the engine users actually use
actual_behavior: The user-facing classifier is the legacy credit-gated /api/analyze pipeline (produces stored P/O/A codes); all vNext candidate-only locks apply to an admin-only path most users never touch
impact: The audited locks do not protect the live user path; risk profile mixes legacy and vNext methodology versions
recommended_action: Confirm legacy /api/analyze non-final posture and copy; decide which engine is canonical for users; align locks

title: Risk Profile combines legacy and vNext P/O/A codes into shared distributions
evidence: server.ts getRiskProfileSummaryForTenant builds perception/objective/action counts from both legacy events.analyses and sera_vnext_analyses; erc.ts computeHfaErcCategoryFromCodes applied to both
expected_behavior: If legacy and vNext used different code spaces or methodology versions they should not be pooled
actual_behavior: Codes are pooled into one distribution assuming code-space compatibility; methodology version differences are ignored
impact: Aggregates may mix methodologically non-comparable classifications
recommended_action: Tag each source with methodology version; either separate distributions or document compatibility

title: Risk score and recurring-pattern aggregates are technical contrivances presented as risk intelligence
evidence: server.ts:528 baseScore = (pTotal*1.0+oTotal*0.8+aTotal*0.6)/totalAnalyses/3*100 with undocumented weights; buildRecurringPatterns has no count threshold (lists count=1); zero-state copy claims 'perfil organizacional' at 10 analyses
expected_behavior: Risk scores/patterns/trends should derive from a documented, methodologically-grounded method
actual_behavior: Arbitrary weighted score (0-100 critical/warning/ok), unthresholded 'recurring patterns', org profile asserted at n=10
impact: Counts dressed as organizational risk inferences; over-claim risk on small samples
recommended_action: Document or replace score method; add thresholds for 'pattern'/'trend'; gate 'organizational profile' on sufficient n; lean on existing data_confidence

title: ERC mapping is a sparse heuristic presented as ARMS ERC
evidence: ARMS_SEVERITY_ROW only maps P-A/P-B/P-F; all else defaults to 'C'; barrier=count of P/O/A != -A; objective/action semantics ignored beyond fail/no-fail
expected_behavior: ERC classification should faithfully implement a validated ARMS mapping
actual_behavior: Two-axis lookup (perception-severity x barrier-count) with 3 defined perception rows; presented as ERC 1-5 risk levels
impact: ERC distribution shown to users is an under-specified heuristic
recommended_action: Validate the ARMS mapping or relabel as a heuristic indicator

title: 176 trial suite is dominated by static text-grep and file-existence guards
evidence: 176 trial files: 96 use readFileSync (93 assert via includes/match/test on file text), 75 use existsSync, only 16 import/execute the engine, 20 use fetch/server, 27 reference supabase
expected_behavior: Headline pass counts should reflect behavioral coverage
actual_behavior: Majority of trials assert presence of strings in source/docs or file existence; ~16 execute the engine
impact: '176/176 PASS' overstates behavioral coverage; many guards pass without exercising audited behavior
recommended_action: Separate static guards from behavioral tests in reporting; consolidate/retire redundant grep guards

title: Public and dashboard copy overstate AI classification and methodology validation
evidence: risk-profile/page.tsx:1680 'A IA classifica os fatores humanos ... com justificativa metodológica'; page.tsx:260 badge 'Metodologia validada'; page.tsx:984 'O HFA/SERA foi validado através de análises comparativas...'
expected_behavior: Copy must match candidate-only / human-review-required posture and actual capability
actual_behavior: Copy states the AI classifies human factors and that the methodology is validated
impact: Communication/methodology risk: contradicts candidate-only locks and the absence of scientific validation; misleads users
recommended_action: Reword to assistive/candidate-only framing; remove or qualify 'Metodologia validada'; align with human-review-required

title: Three overlapping risk endpoints; one is stale legacy-only
evidence: /api/risk-profile and /api/org/intelligence both call getRiskProfileSummaryForTenant (org/intelligence lacks audit log); /api/analyses/risk-profile queries legacy analyses table directly (no vNext, no exclusions)
expected_behavior: One canonical risk endpoint
actual_behavior: Duplicate endpoint (no audit on org/intelligence) plus a legacy-only endpoint that diverges (omits vNext and exclusions)
impact: Divergent numbers if any client uses /api/analyses/risk-profile; inconsistent audit logging
recommended_action: Consolidate to one endpoint; retire /api/analyses/risk-profile; ensure audit logging

title: Risk endpoints leak raw internal error messages to clients
evidence: jsonError(String(e),500) / jsonError(error.message,500) returns internal error text to client (not stack, but DB/internal messages)
expected_behavior: 5xx responses should return a generic message + request id
actual_behavior: Client receives raw error.message on failures
impact: Information disclosure of internal/DB error details
recommended_action: Return generic 500 with request_id; log details server-side only (as product-beta handlers do)

title: Dead engine pipeline retained (engine.ts + legacy steps 01/06-11)
evidence: Product/runtime use runSeraVNextEngineV0; analyzeSeraVNext(engine.ts) only referenced by index.ts and old trials; engine-v0 reuses legacy steps 02-05 only
expected_behavior: Unused pipelines should be removed or clearly quarantined
actual_behavior: engine.ts + legacy steps 01,06,07,08,09,10,11 are dead in product but still exported and exercised by old trials
impact: Confusion about which engine is canonical; maintenance burden
recommended_action: Remove or clearly mark engine.ts pipeline as deprecated; keep only steps reused by engine-v0

title: Tree evidenceRequired/prohibitedInferences are synthesized not sourced
evidence: inferEvidenceRequired/inferProhibitedInferences generate these fields from node-id prefix, not from the canonical asset doc
expected_behavior: Methodological metadata should trace to source
actual_behavior: Generic per-axis strings synthesized at build time
impact: Apparent methodological metadata is not authored/traceable
recommended_action: Source these from the asset doc or mark as engine-generated heuristics

title: O_MANAGED_RISK Portuguese question text vs branch polarity mismatch
evidence: Node O_MANAGED_RISK PT text describes the non-conservative case but SIM->O-A (conservative) and NÃO->O-D
expected_behavior: PT question polarity must match branch semantics
actual_behavior: PT wording and SIM/NÃO leaf mapping are inverted relative to each other
impact: Reviewer confusion; potential mis-answer if a human follows the PT text
recommended_action: Correct the PT question text to match SIM=conservative

title: Escape-point heuristic selects first control-window sentence
evidence: frontend/src/lib/sera-vnext/engine-v0/candidate-escape-window.ts
expected_behavior: buildCandidateEscapeWindow picks earliest sentence containing any control keyword (crew/pilot/decided/continued/...) before first outcome keyword
actual_behavior: Escape point = first moment the operation stopped being safe
impact: Earliest crew-action sentence is taken as escape boundary
reproducibility: Escape point may be placed too early for narratives that open with routine actions
recommended_action: Medium
blocking_status: Refine escape detection toward first safety-relevant deviation; keep PROGRESSIVE_ZONE labeling and human confirmation

title: risk_profile_exclusions lacks DB-level role gate and source FK
evidence: RLS policies check only tenant_id (no admin role); source_id has no FK and no DB-level source-existence/tenant check
expected_behavior: Defense-in-depth: role + source validity enforced at DB too
actual_behavior: Role enforced only at API (requireAdmin) and source existence only at API (universe check)
impact: If any future caller bypasses the API, a non-admin tenant member could write exclusions / orphan source_ids
recommended_action: Add admin role to exclusion RLS; validate source membership in DB or keep API as sole writer and document it

title: Beta JWT helper functions omit explicit search_path
evidence: sera_vnext_beta_jwt_tenant_id/_role/_can_use are SQL STABLE SECURITY INVOKER without 'set search_path'
expected_behavior: Best practice: pin search_path on functions
actual_behavior: No search_path set (mitigated: SECURITY INVOKER, not DEFINER)
impact: Low risk of resolution ambiguity
recommended_action: Add 'set search_path = public' for consistency with get_tenant_id

title: A_CAPABILITY defaults to SIM with no supporting evidence
evidence: decideA A_CAPABILITY returns SIM (statements.slice(0,2)) when no physical/knowledge keywords present, proceeding to A_TIME_PRESSURE
expected_behavior: Each branch answer should be evidence-supported or UNRESOLVED
actual_behavior: Action traversal proceeds past capability without capability evidence
impact: Slightly weakens evidence-first guarantee on the A axis
recommended_action: Return UNRESOLVED when capability evidence is absent

title: Soft-skip try/catch in E2E tests can mask cross-tenant assertions
evidence: Blocked-tenant session creation wrapped in try/catch -> blockedToken=null; subsequent cross-tenant checks may be skipped silently if session setup fails
expected_behavior: Cross-tenant negative tests must fail loudly if preconditions cannot be met
actual_behavior: Setup failure degrades to skipping the blocked-tenant assertions
impact: A failing cross-tenant guard could be reported as pass when the blocked session can't be created
recommended_action: Fail the test when required negative-path session cannot be established

title: Company dashboard uses un-audited /api/org/intelligence
evidence: dashboard/page.tsx:212 fetches /api/org/intelligence which does not write the risk_profile.generated audit event
expected_behavior: Risk-profile reads should be auditable
actual_behavior: Main dashboard reads bypass audit logging present on /api/risk-profile
impact: Incomplete access audit trail for the most-used view
recommended_action: Route dashboard through the audited endpoint or add audit logging to org/intelligence

title: No scientific/empirical validation exists for the methodology engine
evidence: Validation is determinism + boundary compliance on 39 cases (30 synthetic, 9 real); no inter-rater reliability, no empirical accuracy study; public badge 'Metodologia validada'
expected_behavior: Scientific validation requires empirical accuracy and/or inter-rater reliability evidence
actual_behavior: Determinism and contract compliance are demonstrated; scientific validity is not
impact: Claiming scientific/methodological validation is unsupported
recommended_action: Do not claim scientific validation; describe as deterministic implementation of a documented method pending empirical study

title: Documentation sprawl (1287 docs) with microphase and readiness-inflation risk
evidence: find docs/sera-vnext -name '*.md' | wc -l = 1287; pervasive A4R### microphase docs and multiple prior 'audit/readiness' reports
expected_behavior: Living, navigable documentation set
actual_behavior: 1287 markdown files; many superseded microphase artifacts and overlapping readiness/audit reports
impact: High governance burden; conflicting/stale status; readiness inflation
recommended_action: Establish a small living-state doc set; archive superseded A4R microphase docs under an /archive tree

title: Deploy state of c4266d0 (Vercel/staging) not verifiable in this read-only audit
evidence: No authorization/network access to systemhfa.vercel.app or staging DB during this audit
expected_behavior: Confirm deployed commit, migrations applied, flags set
actual_behavior: Cannot confirm what is deployed or which migrations/flags are live
impact: Readiness for environments other than local cannot be asserted
recommended_action: Verify deploy commit, applied migrations, and flag values out-of-band before any pilot
